hiring_signal_brief.signal_summary: Granite Freight is hiring 3 SDRs and a RevOps manager at the same time.
allowed_grounding_points: Granite Freight is hiring 3 SDRs and a RevOps manager at the same time.
Parallel SDR and RevOps hiring can signal scaling pressure.
Pipeline coverage and ramp consistency may be under strain.
disallowed_claims: your reps are missing quota by 30%
we know your sales cycle length

core_value_props: practical coverage while systems catch up
calendar_cta_examples: Worth a brief conversation next week?

prior_thread.speaker: prospect
prior_thread.message: We are busy this quarter and not looking for a big tool rollout.

instructions_to_agent: Reply to the prospect. Acknowledge the objection, stay low-pressure, and connect Tenacious to the signal without overclaiming.

ground_truth.reference_output: Totally fair — I’m not suggesting a big rollout. I noticed Granite Freight is hiring both SDR and RevOps roles at the same time, which usually means the team is balancing pipeline coverage with process cleanup. Tenacious is often useful when a team wants flexible bench support without committing to a larger permanent change. If a short comparison would help, I can send over a calendar link for 15 minutes next week.
must_include: hiring both SDR and RevOps roles
must_avoid: your reps are missing quota by 30%

rubric.signal_grounding: Use the supplied hiring signal and objection context.
rubric.calendar_cta_presence: Include a calendar-oriented CTA if appropriate.
rubric.hallucination_unsupported_claims: Do not invent metrics or insider knowledge.
rubric.segment_fit: Fit the mid-market logistics scaling context.
rubric.overall_score: Weighted average of the six dimensions.

metadata.authoring_notes: Example trace-derived structure using a Week 10 style objection-handling pattern.
